name: Allen Peterson
text:
Edwards Estate is one of the most interesting Wine Estates we visited in the in the Margaret River area.
The full-sized working-state yellow Tiger Moth aeroplane "Matilda" is featured at the entrance inside the building.
The history of the 'plane and owner (Edwards) is documented and very interesting.
The wine guide person was very knowledgeable, as she has worked at Edwards for many years.
The technique of storing wine under the sea after bottling was unique! And then each bottle is left exactly like that, with the sea growth and all, into wooden box with authentication certificate, for collectors items.
The building is made up largely of the local recycled timber, some of the Jarrah wood beams of 100-year old timber.
Great atmosphere.
Yes, well worth visiting!
Recommended!
responseFromOwnerText: Hi Allen, thank you for visiting and for taking the time to leave your lovely review. We hope to see you again!

It was disappointing to be treated that way in such a beautiful setting.
responseFromOwnerText:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your experience was disappointing. We pride ourselves on providing excellent customer service, as reflected in our other reviews, and we want every guest who visits to feel welcomed and looked after.
The staff member who assisted you is new and was not yet familiar with our procedures around tastings close to closing time. We’ll be reviewing this with the entire team to ensure it doesn’t happen again.
We’d love the opportunity to show you the true Edwards Wines experience. Please contact us directly via phone (08 9757 7777) or email (cellardoor@edwardswine.com.au) so we can arrange another tasting on us at a time that suits you.

Warm regards,
Edwards Wines

title: Edwards Wines

Extremely Disappointing Experience
Very poor experience at Edward’s Wines. We arrived 20 minutes before closing hoping for a quick tasting, but were met with rudeness and a complete lack of hospitality. The staff showed no interest in customer service or making visitors feel welcome. For a place in the wine tourism industry, this was incredibly disappointing. I won’t be returning and would strongly recommend avoiding this stop on your next wine tour.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Lachy,

We’re very sorry to hear that your recent visit didn’t reflect the standard of customer service we aim to deliver. Creating a warm and attentive experience for every guest is central to what we do, and it’s disappointing to know this did not happen with your experience.
The team member you met was new, and we apologise that this affected your visit. We’ll ensure all staff receive the proper training so they’re confident in our procedures, particularly when welcoming guests close to closing time.
We value feedback, as it helps us continue to improve. We’d love the chance to invite you back to enjoy the farm, friendly, and fun experience we’re known for — please reach out to us directly so we can arrange a return visit with a tasting on us.

Kind regards,

Edwards Wines
08 9757 7777
cellardoor@edwardswine.com.au

name: Rob Moore
text: Nigel was a very friendly knowledgeable host and his daughter put together a great cheese platter. We were shown the whole set up and the equipment needed to produce and bottle all kinds of wine. The wines were delicious 😋  We sampled lots of different reds and whites at different stages and vintages. Very relaxed and enjoyable experience and came away with a few bottles for memory makers 😍 Thanks again Nigel and Co. Good luck with your future plans to include their own made chocolates. We hope to visit again sometime and sample more of your award winning wines. Great job guys 👏
title: Evoi Wines

name: Kim Kindrat
text: We spent a super relaxing afternoon with Nigel & his lovely Daughter at EVOI Wines. Nigel spent his time with us explaining how his wine is made, from vine to bottle & it was fascinating. That was followed by an incredibly tasty cheese platter 🧀🥂. Evoi Wines are amazing. We don’t usually go for whites but honestly, Nigel has created the most delicious varieties & we enjoyed everything 😋. Huge thank you to Evoi Wines for the best day out!
title: Evoi Wines

name: Steve E
text: Nigel & daughter are passionate about the wines which was evident in the way Nigel made us feel about tasting wines directly from the barrel & tanks. This was followed by a reserve experience at the cellar door. The barrel experience allowed us to understand the artistry & skill that goes into developing cleaverly crafted wines. The barrel wines we could appreciate right now. With cellaring time these would turn more elegant & refined. The wines for me are 1. the chardonnay, not heavily oaked, creamy, lingering apricot lime on the palette. Finish was fine & slight brioche mouth-feel. 2. Malbec, full, fruity without being a bomb & extremely smooth. Slight acidity so would cellar another 5 - 8 years. 3. 2014 the Satyr, smooth rounded soft Bordeaux blend. Surprised at how easy it was to drink now. Cigar box, thyme/oregano, dark berry. Great expression of WA blend. Drinking now or cellar 6 - 8 years. Thanks Nigel for your time in this busy period and we'll be back.
title: Evoi Wines

name: Tommaso Sganzerla
text:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting Evoi Winery and I can confidently say it was an exceptional experience. From the moment I arrived, I was impressed by the warm welcome from the staff.

The tasting room itself was beautifully decorated and cozy, making me feel right at home. The selection of wines was fantastic, and I particularly enjoyed the Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay. The knowledgeable staff were happy to answer any questions I had and even gave me a tour of the production facilities, which was both educational and fascinating.

What really stood out for me, however, was the personalized attention I received from the staff. They took the time to get to know me and my preferences, and made great recommendations for which wines to try based on my tastes. It was clear that they were passionate about what they do and genuinely cared about making my experience enjoyable.

Overall, I had an unforgettable time at Evoi Winery and would highly recommend it to anyone looking for a truly exceptional wine tasting experience. The combination of breathtaking scenery, excellent wines, and attentive staff make this a must-visit destination for any wine lover. Five stars all around!
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Tommaso
Thanks so much for taking the time to write a review.  I'm glad to see the passion of EVOI is showing.  Cheers.

name: Marc Ayden
text:
Fantastic winery to visit in the Margaret River region, Western Australia. Passionate couple who knows a tonne about their wines and are directly involved in every step of the process. They are even able to advise on the taste notes based on the weather and growing season of the year.

Of course, the cute Samoyed Elsa stole the show and was a great hit with the family, greeting us from our car door to the bar counter. Selection of wine are fantastic with a range of reds, whites, sparkling and dessert wines. Our favourite has to be the dessert wine and the Cabernet Sauvignon Reserve 2018 as we're able to taste the complex notes without the strong alcohol masking. We also appreciate that this is a family business and we're directly supporting their great work!

We are proud and happy to say that we maxed out our alcohol allowance for our trip back to Singapore and will be the only winery we visit this time around to Perth.

Kudos to the cellar and the good folks.
responseFromOwnerText: Hi Marc, thanks for calling in and your kind words, come back when you make a trip to Australia again.  Take care.
address: 21 Bessell Rd, Rosa Glen WA 6285, Australia
title: Firetail Wines

title: Fishbone Wines

name: Lily
text:
This has become an annual tradition for our family and the official start of our holiday, usually our first stop before even checking in. This year marked our fourth consecutive visit.

The Japanese (Asian-fusion) food was delicious. They ask upon arrival of anyone has any dietary requirements, which is amazing. Mum and Dad shared the fiery katsu chicken sushi plate, while Dad also indulged in the volcano prawn sushi plate with the special hot sauce. My sister ordered miso soup and edamame, and I had the tonnkotsu ramen. The broth was rich and comforting, though I felt it could have been more generous than just three slices of pork. We also shared the pork gyoza, which were absolutely delicious. For dessert, Dad had the lemon tart, while my sister and I each had a fudge brownie.

All of the food was excellent.

Our server, Vik, was wonderfully kind and patient with our table. She cleared plates efficiently and ensured there was appropriate time between courses. Service was smooth, professional, and well paced.

Before lunch, we participated in a wine tasting. Cho, who we met last year, was absolutely incredible once again. She was extremely knowledgeable and genuinely enthusiastic. She expertly selected wines from the Fishbone range to suit each of us: a sweeter option for myself, a dry palate for my mum, and a varied palate for my dad. I purchased the 2025 rosé, Mum chose the SBS, Dad selected the Shiraz, and Mum and Dad also purchased the Chardonnay Pinot Noir sparkling wine. We purchased 6 wines, with the 15% off deal. The tasting costs $10 per person and includes five wines. Cho is easily six out of five stars, her service is impeccable and she is clearly passionate about both wine and guest experience. Please look after her; she is one of the reasons we chose to return again this year.

Unfortunately, I cannot in good conscience leave a five-star review due to how our visit ended. Both the unisex/disabled bathroom and the women’s bathroom had no functioning soap. The unisex/disabled bathroom was also in an unacceptable state, with toilet paper and liquid across the floor — I have found cleaner facilities at service stations.

After alerting staff, Laura attended and handled the situation extremely poorly. There was no apology, and the concern was dismissed as “the cleaner’s job.” We waited over ten minutes while other patrons approached the bathrooms and turned away due to the lack of soap. When Laura eventually returned, she repeatedly insisted there was already soap in the dispenser, despite it clearly not dispensing. The issue was only resolved when soap was manually pumped into our hands by the the servers.

As a customer experience officer myself, I found this particularly disappointing. In hospitality, you are being paid to remain courteous, approachable, and service-oriented. If a staff member needs a moment, that is entirely understandable — but it should never be taken out on patrons, particularly those spending close to $500 on what is meant to be an enjoyable dining experience. In a food service environment, accessible and functioning handwashing facilities are a basic health and safety requirement, not an optional extra.

Overall, we spent approximately $500 on food and wine and would still return next year. This is a family-friendly restaurant offering a wide range of Japanese-inspired dishes and an extensive drinks list to suit all palates. And if chocolate is your thing and you still have room, the Margaret River Chocolate Factory is conveniently located just across the road.

name: REBECCA DESMOND
text:
A simply magical experience!

From the moment you step out of your car, the experience begins, with elegant, mature gardens leading you to a stunning Dutch South African style building which is home to the Cellar Door and Restaurant.

Every detail has been carefully considered, from plush seats to table locations, providing a truly immersive and private dining experience.

We felt like we were in our own little world, surrounded by beautiful soaring beamed ceilings, wood panelled windows and views across the wonderful gardens. We did not see or hear other guests, although the restaurant was fairly busy....that is how awesome the place and staff made us feel.

The wait staff were simply superb; from explanations about ingredients and wine to every imaginable detail being taken care of with no hesitation. Simply the best service imaginable.

The food, considered as a pairing with the wine, was TOP. The wine, well, it was magnificent.

We cannot wait to come back!

Lunch is a tasting menu with paired wines (small glasses equivalent to 2 full glasses  in total or larger glasses equivalent to 4 glasses) or you can choose by the glass or bottle or just drink water. The food portions were small but delicious, very creative including unusual ingredients and beautifully presented. The service was excellent, attentive and informative concerning the wines and the various dishes - a special mention for Paco our French waiter who made our visit memorable. textTranslated:
This is a very pretty winery housed in a Sato building built in 1996. For the tasting, you can sample five varieties from the list; they usually serve more than usual, but since it was close to the end of the day near 5 PM, I think that was the case.

Although Sadonnay is more expensive among white wines, I find Chenin Blanc to have a longer finesse, be smoother, and have a more suitable aroma for me.

The prices are also on the affordable side. Could it be that it suits my palate, which is accustomed to Korean food?
address: 41 Stevens Rd, Margaret River WA 6285, Australia
title: Voyager Estate

name: Katie Goldberg
text:
🚨 DO NOT VISIT UNLESS YOU WANT POOR TREATMENT FROM STAFF 🚨

This used to be one of our favourite wineries in Margaret River, which makes this experience even more disappointing.

The service has become incredibly impersonal - very commercial, very busy, very meh. Wines are ok but definitely not worth $20 non refundable tasting fee. It no longer feels warm or welcoming while having your tasting, just transactional.

But the real issue: after spending around $200 on tastings, lunch and wine, I was told by a staff member (from the other side from where we were seated) that I couldn’t take a photo of the interior because there were “guests” and that I should come back later. Ummm lol what? The interaction felt so rude and condescending. As a content creator, I’ve honestly never encountered this kind of attitude anywhere else - it’s 2025, not the 90s.

I was… literally a guest. Taking a quick photo with my phone in a pubic setting, not hosting a photoshoot.

The whole approach felt rigid and frankly archaic, especially given how much emphasis wineries place on atmosphere and sharing experiences. Left with a very sour taste. Absolutely horrible.

Won’t be returning - and wouldn’t recommend visiting if you want to leave with anything other than a bad taste in your mouth from the staff treatment. I highly recommend you go to Redgate or Passel estate - they’re incredibly good.

title: Voyager Estate

text: Probably one of the best cellar door and tasting experiences I have had. Highly, highly recommend a visit to Josh and his team.

text: It’s very very rare that I visit a cellar door and enjoy every wine offered to taste but Altair Estate manages it with ease. Highly recommended. Every time I cook a coq au vin from now on I’ll remember this most enjoyable visit.

name: YEYEONG KIM
text:
강력추천👍
따듯한 분위기와 편안한 느낌을 주는 기분좋은 공간이었어요.
사장님 내외분이 정말 친절하시고, 와인과 진,보드카에 대해서 상세하게 설명해주셔서 너무 좋았어요.
맛보여주신 와인들 모두 신선하고 향기로웠고, 특히 보드카는 그동안 마셔본것과는 또 다른 퀄리티 좋은 보드카였어요.
마가렛리버에서 가장 좋았던 경험이었습니다.
(그리고 정말 사랑스러운 강아지도 있답니다😉)
감사합니다 사장님😊
textTranslated:
Highly recommended👍
It was a cozy space with a warm and comfortable atmosphere.
The owners were incredibly friendly and explained the wine, gin, and vodka in detail, which was really nice.
All the wines we sampled were fresh and fragrant, and the vodka, in particular, was of a high quality unlike anything I've tasted before.
It was the best experience I've had at Margaret River.
(And they even have a really adorable dog 😉)
Thank you, owners😊
responseFromOwnerText: Hello Kim, lovely to see you again. Google translated your message!

name: Aliya McNally
text:
After Hours Wine in North Jindong is an absolute gem. Warwick’s hospitality alone is worth the visit, warm, welcoming, and genuinely passionate about what he does. You can tell he loves sharing the story behind every pour.

The limoncello is next level,  perfectly balanced, fresh, and dangerously drinkable. And the vodka? Exceptionally smooth, easily rivaling big names like Belvedere and Grey Goose, if not surpassing them. It’s refined, clean, and impressive from the first sip to the last.

A must-visit for anyone who appreciates quality spirits and wines, great conversation, and an authentic local experience. We’ll definitely be back 🍋🥂
Lara and Emma
responseFromOwnerText: Thanks Aliya and friends, so nice of you to comment. Yes, it was a great afternoon with you. I look forward to welcoming you back!
address: 455 N Jindong Rd, North Jindong WA 6280, Australia
title: After Hours Wine and Gindong Gin

name: Diane Hann
text:
We braced ourselves against the icy weather today and drove to Witchcliffe to taste wines at Amato Vino.
Our hostess was Chelsea, who explained the wines and prepared a delicious cheese platter for us, with local cheese, chutney, olives, quince paste, crackers and grissini.

The wines are quite different to most of the varieties that one usually tries in WA. Nice to see a Marsanne and a white with verdhello amongst its grape varieties. There is quite an Italian style to some of the wines.

At the back of Amato Vino is an old building which is slowly being restored. It is really worthwhile to look at it. It contains a coffee shop which is open in the mornings only. An artist uses another small space there.

A nice little surprise in Redgate Rd, which you might walk or drive straight past, unaware of the activity inside. Take the time to visit!
responseFromOwnerText: Thanks so much for such a lovely, thoughtful review Diane! So glad you enjoyed your visit :🙏

Hi Christopher, 

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We are so glad to hear you enjoyed the atmosphere and our wine selection.

We are very sorry to hear that part of your experience fell short. This isn’t the standard of service we aim to provide, we will follow up with our team to ensure every guest feels genuinely welcomed and looked after.

Thank you again for visiting Amelia Park Wines — we hope to have the chance to provide you with a better experience next time. Please reach out if you plan to visit us again so that we can ensure you have a memorable experience, info@ameliaparkwines.com.au

Thanks,
Daniela

name: Hidesato Ozawa
text: Amelia Park Winery offers a pleasant wine-tasting experience, earning a 3.5/5.0 rating. The interior is luxurious and beautifully designed, creating an elegant and comfortable atmosphere that enhances the visit. The wines are enjoyable, with a decent range of reds and whites, though some selections feel more standard compared to standout wineries. The staff are friendly and helpful, providing guidance on the wines and the tasting process. While the overall experience is positive, the winery doesn’t quite reach the level of excellence found in top-tier wineries. Still, the combination of stylish interiors and solid wine makes Amelia Park Winery a worthwhile stop for visitors in the area.

Been here for tastings multiple times, i love the beautiful wines, particularly the bush vine tempranillo, such a beautiful location, I always bring people with me and recommend it. Our visit today was negatively affected by the middle aged man serving us making misogynistic "men's rights" comments when my friend and I were trying to decide which wine to have next. Distasteful and unprofessional, it has really put me off returning if this type of behaviour to customers is tolerated.

Editing on 30/07 to add that the handling of this review by the winery has been exceptional.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Rachel,
Thanks for your honest feedback, and for being such a loyal supporter of our wines. We are really sorry to hear your recent visit did not reflect the experience you have come to expect from us. The comments you mentioned are unacceptable and not in line with our values or the welcoming environment we strive to create.  We taking this seriously and addressing it interallly. We truly regret that this left such a negative impression and hope you will consider giving us another chance in the future. 
If you would like to discuss this matter future, please reach out and we can make contact info@ameliaparkwines.com.au
Thank you

name: Christine Le
text: I visited Amelia Park on the 24th of March in the afternoon with my family of 13 from overseas to celebrate the marriage of my husband and I. We were very excited for Amelia Park, and from previous experiences it was terrific. A couple of years ago I had tried and loved their Pet Nat. I had been raving about this Pet Nat to my family. When we had our wine tasting I of course asked if they still stocked the Pet Nat and if we could have it as a tasting. The man who served us was an older Australian gentleman who said they didn't anymore. I enjoy natural wines and asked if they stock other natural wines and his response was "We only stock real wines" and left the room passively. At first, I didn't take much notice. But as the tasting went on he was short and dismissive with my family when serving us the tasting. I'm unsure if he had a problem with my family, as we are Asian and we did not know much about wine... hence why we were having a tasting. We went to buy our wines at the end and we ended up paying a decent $300-400. After selecting our wines, we were patiently waiting and my husband asked "Do we get our wines now?" as we were unsure if they were pick up on the spot or delivered and his response was "There is only one of me" and passively went on to get our wines. I am so so disappointed. Brought my family from Finland, New York, Sydney and Melbourne to Amelia Park and they were disappointed with the service we received. Such a shame as we have a large family and friends circle from all over the world, and will not be recommending Amelia Park to anyone else. The wines were nice, but shadowed by the poor service that was racist, unfriendly, passive and short.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Christine,
We want to extend our sincerest apologies that your tasting experience did not meet your expectations. It is always our goal to provide every guest with an unforgettable and enjoyable experience, and we regret that we fell short in your case. 
Please know that we take your feedback seriously and will use it to ensure we improve. We would love another opportunity to make your next visit memorable, please reach out to me so that we can arrange a special tasting for you info@ameliaparkwines.com.au
All the best,
Daniela
title: Amelia Park Wines

name: Melissa Paice
text: Wonderful tasting experience at Amelia Park. The most beautiful cellar door in the south west. Love coming here.
title: Amelia Park Wines

name: Olivier Marill
text: Good wines poor service
responseFromOwnerText: Oliver, we are pleased to hear you enjoyed our wines. We do not think it is fair of you to say you received poor service. You had 11 people in your group tasting wine. We have a small fee for wine tasting, which is redeemable with a wine purchase. We think this is fair and is a practice undertaken by the majority of cellar doors in the region. Of the the 11 people who tasted, 4 people purchased a bottle of wine. Those who did not purchase were charged the fee, which is clearly explained at the beginning of the tasting. Our staff explaining this to you, very kindly, is not poor service.
title: Amelia Park Wines

name: Julie Pym
text:

Celebrated my 60th here at Amelia Park this weekend

We WILL be back …  Amelia Park you’re amazing  x

name: Robert Depadova
text:
Went to Aravina 4 days ago we went as a group of 4. Last year they had a 2 or 3 course menu which was fantastic and on the strength of that we returned.
From getting there this time there was total confusion we booked a table in the restaurant but we're told to go into where it was like a takeaway area no-one came and we were waiting around until someone from behind the bar came around to tell us where we were to sit no front desk for such an elaborate establishment.
Then it got worse we ordered 2 share plates for a starter and 4 prawn linguine for mains, the starter came out and as we were just taking our first mouthful the the 4 linguini came out this raised a red flag.
It came out so quick we had barley ordered 5 mins before and all this food came out
We told the French waiter we didn't want the main yet as we had just started the starter, he made out it was our fault "did we tell the waitress we wanted the starter as a starter" we thought it was obvious we'd ordered starters.
When we got our pasta dish
Being Italian I could tell the pasta  was pre cooked and the sauce was just placed on top, no love at all in the food worst prawn linguini dish I've ever had it was gluggy just one mass of stuck pasta which was chewy $42 awful.
If aravina took as much pride in there kitchen as they do in there gardens and surrounds it would be 5 stars.
Will not go back again, don't waste your money.
It's a fast food restaurant now.
responseFromOwnerText: I’m very sorry to hear about your experience. This matter has been addressed with the team, as it certainly doesn’t reflect the standard of food and service we aim to deliver. Please feel free to email me at venuemanager@aravinaestate.com as we would really appreciate the opportunity to welcome you back and make your experience right.

text:
Great venue/lands. Poorly trained bar staff, with very low to no customer service skills. When asked for allergy and ingredients of a stout. Often contains lactose. Both bar tenders avoided any responsibility and gave no information, and no resolution.
Poor form.

For context :
No one else waiting at the bar.
Just us 3.
Yet, you're staff couldn't give us the time of day to dicuss your drink selection, and tap selection?
And, answer the basic question we had.... ?provide service with a respect, and a bit of cheer ?
If the stout contained lactose. As some don't some do.

Under-whelmed. I expected a higher level of ethical and attentive customer service,  Than what was provided at your venue.

I could not believe the tenacity of a bar tender. To say to us " can you decide? I have other things to do?" My brows raised, as our bar attenders in-attentiveness, causing a level of indecision.
We are paying customers on front of you, attempting to order a drink we want. what do you mean youve got more important things to do?

Poor communication skills from our bar staff. We found it shocking.
responseFromOwnerText: Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We are extremely sorry about your experience. We believe there may have been a misunderstanding, as the staff member was not aware that lactose can be present in some stouts and our beers do change regularly. That said, this is not an excuse and does not reflect the level of service we expect. We have since done additional training with the team around allergens and customer care. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention and hope we have the opportunity to welcome you back for a much better experience.

name: Ross B. Taylor AM
text:
Aravina Estate had just introduced its summer menu on the day we visited this stunning wine-growing estate and restaurant. We were not disappointed.

The menu offered share plates - including squid and a pumpkin salad that were both freshly prepared and tasty - and also main course plates.  My guests ordered the barramundi that was beautifully cooked and presented, and also the prawn, pancetta and chilli linguine. An unusual combination dish, but delicious.  I kept my order simple with a maghuretta pizza; not as good as Italy, but still very nice.
The home-made bread was 'to die for'. Superb.

The wait-staff were all from Europe: France, Italy and Belgium. Professional, charming and engaging. Top marks.

The setting is lovely, completing our joyful lunch experience.

Highly recommended.

title: Barnyard1978

name: Ali Ghandurah
text:
My visit to Barnyard 1978 in Margaret River was an absolute highlight, delivering an experience that was memorable from start to finish. From the moment we arrived, the hostess was simply wonderful. Her welcome was incredibly warm and genuine, and her smile never left her face, making us feel instantly at ease and truly valued. This kind of genuine hospitality truly sets the tone for a fantastic meal.

The ambiance of the restaurant was absolutely magical. Barnyard 1978 offers a stunning setting that perfectly complements the Margaret River region's charm. The atmosphere was captivating, creating a delightful backdrop for our dining experience.

And then, the food was exceptional. We tried several dishes, and each one was prepared to perfection. The duck was outstanding, cooked beautifully and bursting with flavor. The fish dish was equally impressive, fresh and expertly prepared. But the real star was the lamb neck it was incredibly tender, rich, and simply melted in the mouth. Every dish showcased high-quality ingredients and skillful execution.

Barnyard 1978 is more than just a meal; it's a complete dining experience. The combination of the incredibly welcoming hostess, the enchanting atmosphere, and the truly excellent food makes it a must-visit in the Margaret River region. I highly recommend it for anyone looking for a memorable culinary journey in a beautiful setting.

reviewContext.Group size: 3-4 people, Suitable for all group sizes

text:
Unforgettable Culinary Experience.

We had the pleasure of trying the new menu at Barnyard 1978, and it was nothing short of exceptional. Every dish was a perfect blend of bold flavors, fresh ingredients, and thoughtful presentation.
The chef’s creativity really shines through some traditional Peruvian dishes are elevated with modern techniques while still honoring their roots.

From the perfectly seasoned ceviche to the tender chicken, each bite told a story. The balance of spices and textures was masterfully executed. Even the sides and sauces were memorable!

If you’re looking for an authentic yet innovative menu, this is the place. Highly recommend!

name: ssidewayz
text:
It’s not often il write a mediocre review - but Aravina Estate with its all of its elegance and image it portrays fell way short and flat of the mark. My review is 2.5 stars ⭐️ not 3.
Parking is average - the bays are narrow, most people traveling south have 4wd and even for normal sedans it’s a squeeze to get in bays. The gardens and view is really amazing and should be highlighted. The food was the big let down. I ordered the rump cap - slow cooked but the issue was the portion size and speed in which it was delivered. The size was not larger than an average entree and for $46 I expected a lot more. The rustic rosti grated pieces on top were well overcooked. Sweet potato puree was nice. Here’s my whole issue though - this dish was prepared and served not more than 3 mins after we placed our order. It was served before we even got our Glasses or soft drink we ordered.. this raised a red flag and you could taste that the cut of meat had been sitting in a hot bay for sometime before being served. This really frustrates me when places like this clearly prioritize profit over experience. Lucky I had a pizza my partner bought which then filled me up. The pizza was really nice.
Surf museum was a great addition to look over with nice pieces. No cars though like in the magazines aswell so that was also disappointing on top of all the others.
Nice for a look about but won’t rush back here again.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi there,
We’re really sorry to hear that your experience didn’t live up to expectations. We truly appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts. Our rump cap is indeed slow-cooked in advance to ensure tenderness, but it’s finished to order on the grill before being served. That said, we’ll make sure to pass your feedback on to our kitchen team to review.
We’re glad you enjoyed the gardens, views, and pizza, and we hope you might consider visiting again in the future.

name: Chloe Short
text:
We had our wedding at Aravina Estate and while the venue itself was absolutely stunning with beautiful grounds, incredible food, and excellent service from the styling and setup team there were several aspects that left us disappointed.

Our coordinator was rarely present during the getting-ready period, and a few issues added unnecessary stress, including children running near the suite, the area not being blocked off, and equipment not working as expected.

After the ceremony, there was no staff member to guide guests to the cocktail area, and the reception space wasn’t ready when we arrived, leaving our bridal party to finalise setup.

Dinner service was also delayed multiple times despite prior coordination, which pushed our timeline out. There was confusion around our bar arrangements, which caused frustration on the night.

When collecting our decorations, several glass cylinders had been broken despite us providing protective wrapping, and the recovery lunch the following day didn’t meet expectations, with some staff coming across as dismissive.

The venue and food were exceptional, but the coordination and communication throughout the wedding and recovery lunch didn’t reflect the high standard we had hoped for.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Chloe, 

Thank you so much for choosing Aravina Estate to celebrate your wedding. We’re grateful for your kind words about the venue, food, and styling team, but we’re also truly sorry that some parts of your experience didn’t go as you had planned.

We’ve shared your feedback with our team and will be reviewing everything you mentioned. Once again, we’re very sorry.

Kind regards, 
Aravina Team

title: Aravina Estate

text: I’ve been living in Dunsborough for some time now and Aravina has always been a lovely place to visit. The gardens are  beautiful and the venue is peaceful. I took a friend there today after talking up the pizzas, we ordered a surf and turf pizza. My disappointment was palpable when it arrived. There was very little on it and NO seafood apart from two anchovies. The pizza consisted of cheese and some lettuce with a sprinkle of half cherry tomato’s. Worst pizza I’ve ever had. So, so disappointed and embarrassed. Then ordered desert and was served a passion fruit cheese cake which looked like and tasted like it was straight from Cole’s. The staff however were attentive and friendly.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Astrid, 
Thanks for visiting and for your kind words about our venue and team. We were really surprised to read your comment, as there are actually no anchovies on our Surf & Turf pizza — it should have prawns and prosciutto. Something must have gone wrong here, and we’re so sorry about that. Please reach out to us at info@aravinaestate.com so we can make it right.
Kind regards,
Noemie - Venue Manager

name: Jess palmer
text:
We recently stayed at Bettenay’s Margaret River Wine & Nougat and unfortunately had a very disappointing experience.
When we arrived, there was no one at reception for around 20 minutes, which made check-in frustrating. Our suite was located above a restaurant, something that was not mentioned on Booking.com or their website. This lack of transparency was disappointing.

Privacy was a major issue. The only curtains were around the bed area, and people from outside could still see into parts of the room. Even worse, the shower had a visible gap where you can see people outside—and they can see you while you’re showering. This made the stay extremely uncomfortable and felt like a complete lack of privacy.

There was also constant noise from the restaurant below, with voices and music clearly heard until closing time.
The most concerning issue was hygiene. We discovered there were mice when they got into our food, even though it wasn’t left open. This was very upsetting and not what we expected from a stay like this.
Overall, the experience did not meet expectations, and these issues should be clearly disclosed and addressed for future guests.

name: Wolfgang Effenberger
text: Schlechteste Weinverkostung ever. Man durfte immer nur aus zwei verschiedenen Weinen, einen zur Verkostung wählen. Das geht ja gar nicht. Das kann die tolle Lage am See auch nicht wettmachen
textTranslated: Worst wine tasting ever. You were only allowed to choose one wine from two options to taste. That's just unacceptable. Even the beautiful lakeside location couldn't make up for it.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Wolfgang,
First of all i would like to put this in context: you came yesterday with your wife through a tour group (Cheers Wine Tours). Our arrangement with this  tour operator is to give a guided tasting of 4 beverages (3 wines and 1 liquor) for each customer. On top of this we offer the possibility to choose the wines, so that couples/friends can share the glass ending up trying all the wines. I explained this at the beginning of the tasting, so that everyone would know what to expect. When you asked for more wines i reiterated the offer, however I still poured an extra taste. When we arrived at the liquor you asked me to do a double pour of both varieties and i felt that this request was excessive and not fair for the other customers. We are a small family business and we aim to give the best experience to all our customers. However we ask our customers to be respectful with our hardworking staff.
If you come on a tour, i recomend you clarify with your tour guide what is offered at each winery so you are not disappointed.
We are sorry that you were not happy with our tasting, however we are confident in our customer service and we believe we approached this situation in a fair and generous way. 
Regards
Riccardo Losciale (FOH supervisor)

name: Michelle Davey
text: If you are booking the Honeymoon Suite, please be aware that the apartment is located on top of the "restaurant" (or area where people are served food and drinks), and the kitchen. This is not noted on their website or on Booking.com, where we booked this accommodation. We did not feel there was really any privacy or felt comfortable to wait hours until all the customers had left. When this was raised with the Owner, there was no understanding or empathy, and we were angrily told that we "had privacy from 5pm to 10am", which is absurd - not ideal for any couple wanting privacy. Yes, we admit we were very angry at this as we travelled 3.5 hours from Perth to only have to turn around and go straight back home and lost over hundreds of dollars in this booking. We are not saying that this is a deliberate thing they have done, it's just omitted from their website or on Booking.com, because if we had known this information, we definitely would not have booked. This is a lovely location, it's just a real shame that the Owner behaved in such an uncaring and combative manner. Very disappointing unfortunately and not the way we wanted to start our 28-year wedding anniversary.

name: Tonatiuh Vasquez
text:
Having lunch at Barnyard 1978 was an absolute delight from start to finish. Every dish I tried was bursting with flavor and crafted with precision. But the true standout was Chef Agustin’s Ceviche. The tender seafood, bright citrus marinade I’m still dreaming about. Agustin’s talent for balancing bold, zesty flavors with subtle sophistication is undeniable, and his passion for food shines through in every bite.

Beyond the exceptional cuisine, the warm, welcoming service made the experience even more special. The staff was attentive and genuinely friendly, offering thoughtful suggestions without ever feeling overbearing. Pair that with reasonable prices and Barnyard 1978 becomes a rare gem where value meets excellence. Whether you’re a ceviche devotee or simply seeking a meal that feels both elevated and satisfying, this spot deserves a spot on your must-visit list. Bravo to the entire team—I’ll be back soon!

name: Caitlin Ferrie
text:
We visited Brown Hill Estate as our first stop on our tour with Cheers Scenic and Winery Tours. Brown Hill was by far my favourite stop. If you are looking for a winery to stop by, learn about and taste a few wines then look no further, you've found it!
As a small family owned winery they have a clear passion for making quality wines and the extensive knowledge and skills to bring that to fruition.
Our tasting was led by the lovely Gwen who took us on an introductory journey through the world of wine. We learnt how to properly taste wines and about the production processes of wines. While we likely only touched the surface of Gwen's expertise, I left knowing so much more about how different grapes, picking times and processes produce different wines and can change the flavour profiles.
Brown Hill Estate is a must-visit spot in Margaret River - whether you attend on a tour or just stop in, you are guaranteed to leave happy (and likely with a few bottles). Very highly recommend!
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Caitlin,
What a wonderful thing to read on a Saturday morning! Thank you for visiting and for taking the time to share your experience. We hope we'll see you again before long.
Best,
Chiara Bailey

name: Oliver Theis
text:
Wir sind gerade auf einer Rundreise durch WA und hatten heute Gelegenheit in der Gegend um Margaret River ein paar Weingüter zu besuchen und deren Wein zu verkosten. Mit weitem Abstand die beste, kundenfreundlichste, warmherzigste und professionellste Verkostung haben wir mit Nic im Brown Hill Estate erlebt.
Sie ging auf die Wünsche und Vorlieben jedes Einzelnen ein und erklärte uns auf sehr charmante Weise nebenbei noch die Details zu jedem einzelnen Wein. Da fühlt man sich verstanden und gut aufgehoben.
Wenn dann noch, wie hier, die Qualität der Weine passt, dann kauft man auch gerne ein paar Flaschen, auch wenn - ein kleiner Wermutstropfen - die Preise schon im oberen Segment angesiedelt sind. Mein persönliches Highlight war der Bill Bailey Shiraz. Wow!

Wer immer hierin die Gegend kommt, dem empfehle ich aus voller Überzeugung einen Besuch bei Brown Hill.
👍👍👍👍👍👍
textTranslated:
We're currently on a road trip through Western Australia and today we had the opportunity to visit a few wineries around Margaret River and taste their wines. By far the best, most customer-friendly, warmest, and most professional tasting we experienced was with Nic at Brown Hill Estate.

She catered to everyone's wishes and preferences and charmingly explained the details of each wine along the way. It made us feel understood and well taken care of.

When the quality of the wines is also excellent, as it was here, you're happy to buy a few bottles, even if—a slight drawback—the prices are on the higher end. My personal highlight was the Bill Bailey Shiraz. Wow!

I wholeheartedly recommend a visit to Brown Hill to anyone who's ever in this area.

👍👍👍👍👍👍
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Oliver,
Thank you — this really does make it all worthwhile. Brown Hill is very much a family effort and hearing that guests love their experience here is the best possible reward.  Nic is an absolute delight and we are so fortunate to have her.
Best,
Chiara Bailey

Highly recommend this spot!

name: EM
text:
Myself and my large group of family just visited this winery. Our experience was not great.
We were chatting between eachother and were told “I will wait until everyone is ready” and the host was banging the bottle of wine on the table insinuating for us to be quiet. She then proceeded to say “guess my profession prior to this” we all guessed teacher as she was still acting like a teacher to adults who paid for a wine tour. A few of my family members who enjoyed the nougat liqueur asked for another sip as they were prepared to buy a few bottles of this and some wine; they were told no and not in a polite way. This ruined the mood and we all decided to leave immediately as her behaviour throughout this wine tasting was horrible. So disappointed as we have been here prior and had a lovely experience and bought numerous bottles of wine. We also had to stop her from proceeding with the wine tasting as we were waiting for 2 more people, she continued to speak and was annoyed that we were talking whilst she was. Overall a bad experience unfortunately.
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Elisha,

It is not often we choose to respond to reviews, however here goes. Your review has prompted us to reassess our relationship with our chosen tour providers. Will we accept large groups in the future? I'm not too sure!

We have been entertaining tour groups for the past 20 years and it is truly disheartening when our guests choose to listen or show an interest in our business and products. We appreciate that you may have paid to have a driver for the day, however we DO NOT get paid for the wine that you taste. We offer a complimentary wine tasting to the guests of our chosen tour operators, as we have built a relationship with them over the years. What we get in exchange for offering complimentary tastings is the opportunity to showcase and sell our products. It is very difficult to present our products to larger groups, especially when everyone is talking amongst themselves. We need to promptly get everyone's attention as the buses are on a time schedule and have bookings at other locations, as do we have other bookings and customers. We also need to consider the experience of our customers, as well as our team members who are doing their best to present to a large group. 

We are one of the few venues that do accept larger groups, however you may find in the future that it is difficult to find any venues accepting larger groups if there is a disrespectful culture.

We are a small family business and your 1 star review was unnecessary and harmful to the future of our business. 

We do hope you enjoyed your tour and that in the future you may have a different perspective when entering a wine business or any business that has willingly accepted a large group and you may show some empathy and maturity.

text: We had a fantastic visit to this family-owned winery and couldn’t recommend it highly enough. She explained everything in a really down-to-earth way and shared so much knowledge about the wines without it ever feeling overwhelming or scripted. You can really feel the passion behind the farm and the wines they produce. Chiara’s enthusiasm and warmth made the experience truly special, and we left with an even greater appreciation for the wine and the people behind it. A must-visit if you’re in the area!
responseFromOwnerText:
Bless you Cian for such a gorgeous review.  Such kind words.
Best,
Chiara Bailey

name: Michael Mok
text:
Made it my mission to visit and try small batch wineries in the South West. Researched upon a common name among the locals.. 'the shed winery' in the forest.

Cape Grace Wines popped up a few times after scouring the net for some local gems.

A huge barrel with the name Cape Grace Wines greeted us at the entrance. With no overly fancy renovation, 'artsy' stuffs, museums and a grand 10 staff members, instead the wonderful Rachel greeted us among the boxes of wines at the entrance. We immediately realise this is a no frills winery, nothing but good wines.

Knowledgeable Rachel started educating us on the regions' wines, the varietal of grapes, and tasting notes of at least 9 different types of wines.

Great Chardonnays, cabernets and shiraz. So decided to invest in their 2019 cabernet sauvignon magnum to open in the next 10 years.

An absolute need to visit when you are in this region.
responseFromOwnerText: Hi Michael, thank you for sharing your experience at Cape Grace.  Rachel loves sharing her knowledge, hope she also took you out the back and you were able to see the red grapes fermenting.  Love the photos you took too!  Enjoy your Magnum in 10 years...if you can't wait that is okay.

text: Tasting was fine. But when we politely asked if we could use the back entrance to cycle to Xanadu, were told staff were too busy to help. Asked again but were told “we have no obligation to open the gate” … wrong attitude in my view
responseFromOwnerText:
Hi Paul
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re glad you enjoyed the tasting, but we are very sorry to hear that our communication regarding the back gate fell short of the hospitality we strive for. For safety and security reasons, we usually keep that gate restricted, but there is no excuse for a dismissive attitude. We will certainly discuss this with our team to ensure we’re handling these requests with more care in the future. We hope to have the chance to welcome you back for a better all-around experience.

Kind regards
The Cape Mentelle Team
address: 331 Wallcliffe Rd, Margaret River WA 6285, Australia

name: Taste Tycoon
text: We had the premium wine tasting, $25 per head. Nice service from the lady, but unfortunately the tasting fee is not redeemable upon purchase of wines, which is common practice in MR. We decided not to purchase anything for this reason and it's off the list on future visits.
responseFromOwnerText:
HI Taste Tycoon,
Thank you very much for taking the time to share your feedback. I’m glad to hear you enjoyed the service from our team member, though I’m sorry to learn that our tasting fee policy impacted your experience.

At Cape Mentelle, our Premium Tasting is designed as a curated, seated experience showcasing a selection of our estate wines. For this reason, the $25 fee is not redeemable on purchase. We understand this differs from some other venues in Margaret River, and we appreciate that this may influence personal preferences when visiting.

Your feedback is genuinely valuable to us, and I will be sure to pass it on to our management team for review. We do hope you’ll consider visiting us again in the future so we can provide a more enjoyable experience.
Kind Regards
The Cape Mentelle Team

text: Beautiful tasting room and excellent staff. However BE WARNED: Tasting fee NOT redeemable with purchase. Decided against purchasing for that reason.

Regarding the tasting fee, we understand your concern. At Cape Mentelle, we focus on providing a guided and curated tasting experience, which is why the fee is not redeemable on purchase. We do, however, hope you’ll consider returning in the future to explore more of our wines and experiences. Your feedback is appreciated and helps us continually refine the way we welcome our guests.

name: JackieFM
text:
There are a couple of reasons for my 3 star rating. Firstly we had visited some amazing wineries in the Margaret River Region in the past week and were greeted by very knowledgeable and friendly staff, we did not get the same attitude from Cape Mantelle staff. We were sat at a table and basically forgotten about? I think separating people on tables in the back room does not make for a friendly atmosphere and makes it more difficult for your staff to move around to customers efficiently.
We were also not impressed that it seemed to be more important to charge people $15 for 50ml tasting then selling wine. I decided not to purchase 2 $60 bottles of wine out of principle as you didn't waiver the tasting fee like the other wineries we had visited and purchased from.
Perhaps that might be something in the future to consider.
We did purchase 2 of your beautiful candles.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Jackie
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear that your experience with us didn’t meet your expectations, especially after visiting other wineries in the region. We always aim to provide friendly, attentive service, and it’s disappointing to learn that we fell short during your visit.
We appreciate your comments regarding the tasting format and fees. Our tasting experience is designed to showcase a wide range of wines with guidance from our team, and the $15 fee reflects this. We do understand your perspective, and your feedback is valuable as we continue to review and refine our offering.
We’re glad to hear you enjoyed our candles, and we do hope you’ll consider visiting us again in the future so we can provide the welcoming experience we strive for.
Kind Regards
The Cape Mentelle Team

name: Jenny Doyle
text: I can’t comment on the owners of this place because I never met them but I can say Debbie is one of the rudest people I have met on an organised tour ever. We went here as part of a wine tasting tour and Debbie made us all feel very uncomfortable and almost like children who had done something wrong. She complained to our bus driver that she wouldn’t continue the wine tasting (which we had paid for!) unless everyone stopped talking. The group we were in had no problem not talking while the different wines were being explained but Debbie never told us when she was doing anything so how were we to know if she was having a chat with someone or explaining a wine! She even made a rude side comment or two a couple of times towards people and just really put a dampener on the afternoon! I know myself and a good few others were going to purchase a bottle or two (particularly the rose which was delicious by the way) but after this we didn’t bother as we felt it wasn’t going to be appreciated there. I will say the varieties of wine were all lovely and the vineyard itself is beautiful (especially with all the furry friends running around) but Debbie really didn’t have to act the way she did. Maybe had it have been with the owners themselves or with someone who is good with showcasing the property and the wines they have to offer to a group it might have been different experience.
title: Cape Naturaliste Vineyard

Full marks to Jen for being a fantastic host, but it's the two doggies, Poppy and Barney, that gives the place its charm.

name: Jessica Pringle
text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

We’ve been coming to Cape Naturaliste Vineyard for years, and it truly feels like a second home. From the moment you arrive, you’re made to feel genuinely welcome — our dogs’ photos are even on the walls, and the dog treats always flow generously! 🐾

As if that wasn’t enough, the wine is simply fantastic. Today, the Shiraz sparkling completely captured my heart — absolute standout and a must-try.

Warm hospitality, a dog-friendly atmosphere, and exceptional wine make this place something really special. We’ll keep coming back, again and again. 🍷

name: Sallyanne Hay
text:
We absolutely loved our visit to Cape Naturaliste Vineyard — so much so that we came back the very next day so Mollie could play with Barney. The vibe here is relaxed, welcoming and beautifully dog friendly, which makes it an easy favourite for anyone travelling with dogs.

Our heeler, Mollie, was allowed inside the cellar door with us. She got her own Polaroid photo taken and added to the famous dog wall.

The wines are fantastic (the views over the vines and flower beds don’t hurt either!). It’s rare to find a cellar door that caters so well to both humans and pups.

A must-visit in the South West — we’ll be recommending it to every dog-loving traveller we meet! Our highlight from the area🍷🐶💛
responseFromOwnerText: Aw we love your fab review, thankyou. So happy you enjoyed your visit to our vineyard.  We loved having you &  beautiful Molly.  Hope to see you back sometime.

text:
We visited Cape Mentelle and booked the Behind The Scenes Tour with Food & Wine Pairing through Tripadvisor. It ended up being just the two of us that day, so we had a private tour—which made the experience even more special.

Our guide, Mary, was incredibly kind and knowledgeable. She took her time walking us through the winemaking process and sharing insights about the vineyard’s history.

The wine tasting and food pairing were thoughtfully curated—each combination brought out the unique flavours of the wines beautifully. It was clear a lot of care had gone into the pairings.

Overall, a fantastic experience from start to finish. Highly recommended for anyone visiting the Margaret River region!
responseFromOwnerText:

It’s wonderful that the tour turned into a private one—what a great way to explore the winery in a more intimate setting! We’ll be sure to pass along your lovely feedback to Mary; she’ll be thrilled to know she made such a positive impression.
We're also glad to hear you enjoyed the curated pairings and the attention to detail throughout your visit.
We truly appreciate the recommendation and hope to welcome you back again next time you’re in Margaret River!

The wine tasting was incredible too. Every glass, from start to finish, was smooth and beautifully balanced.
Overall, it was a really enjoyable experience. We had a great time and would highly recommend it to anyone interested in wine or just looking for a relaxing day out.

name: Dave J
text:
Beautiful estate, just a short drive from Margaret River. The cellar door and grounds are excellently maintained.

Some of the wines are worth the money, especially the reds. The Chardonnay is overpriced for what else is readily available in the region.

We were a bit put off that the tasting fee does not come off a purchase, even if you spend $$$ and out of 10 wineries they had the most expensive postage fee.

Both our hosts in the barrel tasting room were lovely and we got chatting about their experiences, the wine industry and Margaret River, in addition to the tasting notes.
The iPad tasting notes are very detailed for people who prefer to guide themselves.

We will continue to purchase the Cabernet Sauvignon back home as it is an exceptional wine!
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Dave, 
Thank you for taking the time to share your detailed feedback—it’s greatly appreciated. We’re so pleased to hear you enjoyed the estate, and that our team made your visit to the barrel tasting room a memorable one. It's great to know you appreciated both the conversations and the self-guided tasting notes.

We understand your thoughts regarding the tasting fee and postage costs. While our structure reflects the premium experience your feedback is valuable and will be taken into consideration as we continue to refine our offerings.

It’s fantastic to hear you’ll continue to enjoy our Cabernet Sauvignon—it truly is a special wine, and we’re glad it’s found a place in your collection.

Kind Regards

text:
Been to a few of the bigger and more commercialised vineyards, but we were blown away by this guided tour (Behind the Scenes of Wine Making), by Ben at Cape Mentelle. His passion for the industry made a whole difference during our 90 mins with him. He was very engaging, and shared many details in layman's terms, where my parents could understand too.

During the wine tasting, 🍷 the ambience and atmosphere was very intimate. Ben was there explain each of the wine and its background. He also related back to what we saw during the tour, such as the type of ageing process, etc.

We had a great time together! Bought a bottle of their premium wine and enjoyed it on our last night at Margaret River.

Thank you for the amazing experience! Highly recommended! As its a relatively smaller vineyard, I would recommend to book in advance for the guided tour.
responseFromOwnerText:

text: We had an incredible time trying wines today at Cape Mentelle. This is not your average fast, over the counter tasting but a relaxed, lovely experience in a beautiful barrel filled room with an incredible explanation from Mateo. If you want to slow down a bit and try some exquisite wines in a lovely atmosphere this is the place to go!
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Natalia, Thank you for your wonderful feedback! We're so glad to hear you had an incredible experience at Cape Mentelle and enjoyed the relaxed tasting in our barrel room. Matteo will be thrilled to know his insights made your visit even more special.

We truly appreciate your support and hope to welcome you back again soon for another memorable tasting!
address: 331 Wallcliffe Rd, Margaret River WA 6285, Australia
title: Cape Mentelle

name: Connor
text: Loved this winery on our visit to the south-west. It has a really relaxed feel and is set close to the beach. The staff were lovely and we especially loved the dogs and dog Polaroids around the winery. They had a great selection of whites and we especially loved the dessert wine. Will definitely be back!
responseFromOwnerText: Thank you for a fantastic review. So happy you enjoyed our winery & wines & would love to see you back again sometime. 👍🙏
title: Cape Naturaliste Vineyard

text: Super down to earth. Dog friendly. Super genuine owners. Absolute bliss. Also, soooo many beautiful flowers out the front and surrounding? More than one ever seen at any winery/vinyard. I took so many photos of the roses and other flowers! Honestly so beautiful and relaxed here, plus super lovely crisp whites and beautiful reds. This will be my regular stop now.
responseFromOwnerText: Thankyou for an amazing review. So glad you appreciated & enjoyed our wines & venue. We love our flowers too.⚘️🙏👍

responseFromOwnerText: Thankyou so much for a great review. We agree we think the reds are great too. 🍷😋😋

The views spoke for themselves with green rolling hills this time of year. A highlight was the wines which showcase the region’s best. We left with bottles in hand and smiles on our faces, already looking forward to our next trip down south. This gem is a must-visit for anyone who appreciates great wine and genuine hospitality. Can't wait to return!

name: Justyna
text: Super ciepła i przyjazna obsługa, pyszne wina. Przed przyjazdem do Australii polecam wyrobienie sobie passport Wine, dzięki któremu możecie dostać fajne rabaty przy zakupie win w określonych miejscach lub darmową degustację. Ten paszport zmotywował nas do odwiedzenia wielu lokalnych winiarni i zbierania pieczątek, podpisów, pysznych win i wspomnień. Polecam to miejsce
textTranslated: Super warm and friendly service, delicious wines. Before coming to Australia, I recommend getting a Wine Passport, which allows you to get great discounts on wine purchases at certain locations or a free tasting. This passport motivated us to visit many local wineries and collect stamps, signatures, delicious wines, and memories. I highly recommend this place.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Antoine G
text:
A charming wine cellar, nestled in the countryside not far from Capel.
The establishment is located into the vineyard, you can enjoy a great view while tasting your wines!
About the tasting, you can appreciate all their wines vertically for $10, and honestly, it's worth it in the good way.
I personally found the Sauvignon Blanc, Sémillon and Chardonnay very refined and elegant. And of course they've heaps of choices (Pet Nat, Shiraz, cab sav, Merlot...)
There is a large reception area that can accommodate large tables, but it is no longer possible to dine-in since Covid. A small cheese/cold meat platter would be more than welcome!
Otherwise, it is a wonderful place with an excellent selection of wines and a good customer service.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Georgia Pitt
text: We had a lovely tasting of the whites and rose, and because it was nice and quiet were able to have our dog on the lawn while we did a tasting outside. We’d definitely visit again, thank you so much for such fantastic service!
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Jason Holmes
text: My favourite winery, first of many great stops on the way to Margaret River, great reds, a great to stop to slow everything down before you head further south
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: TOMIO YAMAZAKI
text:
フレンドリーでホスピタリティにあふれるスタッフのおかげで、とても気持ちの良い時間を過ごせました。開店と同時に訪れ、5人の友人と一緒にワインテイスティングを体験。10ドルで9種類ものワインを試せ、さらにワインを購入すればテイスティング代が無料になるのも嬉しいポイントです。

特に2016年のシラーズは素晴らしく、全員が購入しました。葡萄畑を見下ろす絶景も圧巻で、ワインの味わいを一層引き立ててくれます。

バッセルトンからわずか20分とアクセスも良好。パースからバッセルトンに向かう際や帰路に立ち寄るのに絶好のロケーションです。ぜひおすすめしたいワイナリーです
textTranslated:
Thanks to the friendly and hospitable staff, we had a very pleasant time. We visited right when they opened and experienced a wine tasting with five friends. For just $10, we could sample nine different wines, and the fact that the tasting was free if we purchased wine was a great bonus.

The 2016 Shiraz was particularly outstanding, and we all bought some. The breathtaking view overlooking the vineyards was also spectacular, further enhancing the taste of the wine.

It's conveniently located just 20 minutes from Busselton, making it an ideal stop on the way to or from Perth. I highly recommend this winery.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Tara
text: Our visit today was on a quieter day, lucky for us. The ramp up the entwined vines archway had a lovely feel, dogs are allowed however not in the Cellar Door itself. We were instantly welcomed into the  Cellar Door by Emma,  already assisting other guests with tastings & purchases Emma made time to guide us through an informative tasting & made our visit memorable. There was no restaurant to order food from, available was a lovely array of goodies to purchase, including award-winning & GF crackers with an assortment of spreads & relishes, you could take home or enjoy with a bottle or glass of your most enjoyed wine by the open fire or vineyard view from any of the tables inside or out. Thank you Emma for a lovely experience we will be back.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Meg Howard
text: Had a lovely wine tasting today! The location is incredible, the wines were great, the lady who did the tasting (sorry I didn’t catch her name) was fantastic! She gave a small spiel about each wine and was very friendly. We left with 3 bottles of wines. Overall a great experience, I can’t wait to come back in the summer months when the vines are green.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Desiree Low
text:
Spent a lovely afternoon at Capel Vale Wines. Emma invited us to have a seat while she brought every course of the tasting menu to us, shutting between guests at the counter and us who were reading and soaking up the lovely views while tasting their variety of wines.
A great part of the experience was being able to compare different shirazs or different cab sauvs from the same label.
As sit in customers we also got to purchase cheeses to accompany our wines. Emma set us up with a cheeseboard, knives, plates and napkins, and really engaging conversations about the wines and her experiences living in Busselton.
One regret was we couldn't take any Capel ducks with us (though we did leave with a good bottle of our favourite wine). Pls consider making cards with your coloured label artwork, or plushies of your green, gold and white duck, or good quality wooden ducks - like the oriental mandarin duck ornaments, classy and display worthy. I'll be back for a duck if you launch them.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Sophie
text: Such a lovely winery with beautiful views and great wines.  Fantastic service from Mel, who was very knowledgable and was able to tailor the tasting to our specific styles.  We had a lovely experience here and would highly recommend.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Christie McCabe
text:
A perfect escape from the everyday.
We had such a lovely visit to this beautiful winery. Emma gave us a warm welcome, and we thoroughly enjoyed relaxing in the comfortable setting overlooking the vineyard. The wine tasting was excellent—each pour thoughtfully paired with wine-friendly cheese and crackers. We learnt the history of the wines and their distinctive flavours. A truly delightful experience. Highly recommend making a stop here.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Alison Martin
text:
Took family there as they really wanted to take some wine back as a gift to Tassie.  Said they were open on google, but they weren't. I rang and spoke to manager and said how unimpressed we were as my visitors wanted to buy 2 cases of wine.
He apologised etc. Said that google always got it wrong.
Fair enough
But then I asked him why he still had the open sign out on the side of the road. He said he would look into it.
Then they order online, they returned to Tassie on 10th Nov, boxes arrived 16 Dec.
It appears it took more than 2 weeks before they even dispatched them. I cannot fathom how this is at all fair or reasonable.  Morale of the story? Don't be in a hurry for your wine. My family did not have the best experience and were not impressed with the slow service
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Carol A
text: Beautiful wines, beautiful winery. Shame there's no restaurant here any more though.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Rhonda M
text:
An AMAZING wine tasting experience. Mel and Genevieve were so kind and knowledgeable of the Capel wines. I did a white wine tasting and really enjoyed it all.

The most surprising of the wines were the Chardonnay and the sparkling rose. Mel told me to keep a little of the Chardonnay in my mouth for 10 seconds and it changed the taste. And it did! It is so good.

The sparkling rose is naturally fermented and the carbonation was also natural. It was a cute name What the Duck ha ha.

The roses and purple butterfly bushes at the entrance are a breathtaking view. A gorgeous property and fun place to sample some excellent dry white wines.  They have reds as well but I am just a white wine fan.

Thank you for making my day!
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: John G
text: NEVER OPEN! Been here 4 times in the last 2 weeks and it's always closed!!! No staff around. Signals says open as does website and google. Won't be back.
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Robert Taylor
text:
Im not going to give a bad review for this but cellar door isn't open Tuesday Wednesday or Thursday with a sign posted on the front door. Yet Google and a sign out the front say otherwise!

Wines are excellent though and ill return one day when you're actually open but please update your google hours to save people the trip out 🙏
title: Capel Vale Wines & Cellar Door

name: Martyna
text: It was very disappointing to find out we still had to pay for tasting despite purchasing two bottles totalling $165. It ruined the experience as this is the first time I have ever been charged for a tasting despite purchasing the bottles from the tasting. I would not recommend.
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Martyna,  
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. I’m sorry to hear about your disappointment with the tasting charges, even after your purchase of two bottles. We aim to provide a memorable and enjoyable experience for all our guests, and it’s clear that we didn’t meet your expectations this time.  

Our tasting policy, including fees, is designed to align with industry standards, but I understand how this might have affected your experience. Your feedback is invaluable, and I will ensure it’s shared with the team for review.  
If there’s anything we can do to improve your experience in the future, please don’t hesitate to let me know.  
Kind regards,  
Cape Mentelle Cellar Door Team
address: 331 Wallcliffe Rd, Margaret River WA 6285, Australia
title: Cape Mentelle

name: Bernie Herbert
text:
My wife & I visited Cape Mentelle Winery in the Margaret River region in December, 2024 and took the food & wine matching tour.
Our Tour Guide was Ed, a really friendly, likable bloke who explained the whole wine-making process (from vine to table) very ably & clearly. Ed is passionate about wines & his enthusiasm is genuine.
We have both been on similar Tours elsewhere over the years but learned new information from Ed.
Ed welcomed our questions and is a great asset to Cape Mentelle.
Written 21 December 2024
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Bernie,
Thank you for taking the time to share your experience! We’re delighted to hear that you and your wife enjoyed the food and wine matching tour during your visit to Cape Mentelle.

It’s fantastic to know that Ed made such a positive impression. He truly is passionate about what he does, and we’re thrilled that his enthusiasm and knowledge added value to your tour. We’ll be sure to pass on your kind words to him—he’ll be so pleased to know he made your visit memorable.

We hope to welcome you back again soon for more great experiences!
Kind Regards

text: We had the best tasting experience at Cape Naturaliste wines! Loved being able to take our dog Woody with us and we were greeted by the owners two friendly pups. Woody was given some treats, had his photo taken and sat inside the A/C with us while we sampled the reds, which were divine! Such a beautiful and fun concept — definitely worth a visit while in the south west! 🤎
responseFromOwnerText: Thankyou so much. Glad you enjoyed your visit as much as we enjoyed your company. Especially your cute doggy Woody 🩵

text: Nice boutique vineyard.

text: Had a great time at Cape Naturaliste last weekend. Deb was very knowledgeable on the different wines and was great to chat to. The Merlot was gorgeous! Highly recommend

One of the best winery experiences of our south west stay. Deb and Craig are incredibly knowledgeable, passionate
humorous individuals who kept us thoroughly engaged throughout our visit.  Oh.... and their wines are outstanding.
responseFromOwnerText: Thanks so much. Glad you enjoyed your visit. Hope to see you again. 👍

name: Christine Cork
text: We visited as part of a small tour with Wine for Dudes on Saturday 22/2. It was just fantastic- great vibe and wines. Authentic and down to earth.Loved the winery dogs and we learnt a lot from Jen who kept us laughing while we were learning.And we bought some amazing wine to bring home.
responseFromOwnerText: So glad you enjoyed your visit. Hope to see you back again. Jen. 👍
title: Cape Naturaliste Vineyard

name: Michael StJohn
text: Just finished my first experience at the Vineyard cellar door. Thank you to Jen and the team for taking us on a fabulous winemaking education and a generous wine-tasting journey. It’s an absolute pleasure to connect with people so passionate about their craft, equalled by the quality of their product and commitment to the customer experience. You guys were wonderful, I look forward to the arrival of our mail order and wish you every success.
responseFromOwnerText: Thankyou for visiting & so glad you enjoyed it. Look forward to seeing you on your next visit. 👋

text: We went for a wine tasting and learned a lot of information. Jen was very kind and knowledgeable about each of the wines and how they get their taste. She was very fun and made it a great experience!
responseFromOwnerText: Thankyou for calling in. Glad you enjoyed your vist.  Hope to see you again. 👍👍
